chapter_title.en: The Limits of Ablution, Its Order and Reward

text: 106 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ اَلرَّجُلُ فَلْيَصْفِقْ وَجْهَهُ بِالْمَاءِ فَإِنَّهُ إِنْ كَانَ نَاعِساً فَزِعَ وَ اِسْتَيْقَظَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلْبَرْدُ فَزِعَ فَلَمْ يَجِدِ اَلْبَرْدَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.106 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"'When a man performs ablution, let him splash his face with water, for if he is drowsy, it will awaken him, and if it is cold, he will not feel the cold.'".
